caption: Compute Environment
description: The Compute Environment object describes the runtime execution environment in which an event originated. This includes serverless functions, container orchestration platforms, edge compute nodes, and traditional server environments.
name: compute_environment

cloud.description: The cloud provider and region of the compute environment.

container.description: The container in which the workload is running.

desc.description: A description of the compute environment.

name.description: The name of the compute environment. For example, a Lambda function name, ECS cluster name, or Kubernetes namespace.

provider.description: The compute platform provider. For example: AWS Lambda, Azure Functions, Google Cloud Run.

region.description: The deployment region of the compute environment.

type.description: The type of compute environment, normalized to the caption of the type_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.

type_id.description: The normalized compute environment type identifier.

1.caption: Server
1.description: A traditional server or virtual machine.

2.caption: Serverless
2.description: A serverless function (e.g., AWS Lambda, Azure Functions).

3.caption: Container
3.description: A containerized workload (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes pod).

4.caption: Edge
4.description: An edge compute node (e.g., AWS Greengrass, Azure IoT Edge).

5.caption: Embedded
5.description: An embedded or IoT compute environment.

uid.description: The unique identifier of the compute environment. For example, a Lambda function ARN or ECS task ARN.

version.description: The version or runtime version of the compute environment.
